methods.description: General procedure for synthesizing 1,1,1-trifluoroalkanones using a Grignard reaction followed by purification steps.

steps.description: A dry 2-necked round-bottomed flask attached to a condenser was filled with magnesium (8 mmol, 2 equiv) under argon and stirred for 30 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare magnesium under argon atmosphere for Grignard reaction

steps.description: An iodine crystal was added. The mixture was warmed gently and stirred vigorously for 10 minutes.
Dry diethyl ether (10 mL) was added followed by the dropwise addition of 1,1,1-trifluoro-4-iodo-butane (4 mmol, 2 equiv) at such a rate as to maintain a gentle reflux.
steps.searchable_summary: Add diethyl ether and trifluoro-iodo-butane to maintain reflux

steps.description: After the addition was complete, the mixture was stirred at reflux for an additional 30 minutes.

This solution of 4,4,4-trifluorobutylmagnesium iodide (10 mL, 2 equiv) was added to a solution of the corresponding benzonitrile (4 mmol, 1 equiv) and CuBr (0.2 mmol, 5.0 mol %) in dry THF (10 mL) at 0 °C under argon.
steps.searchable_summary: Add trifluorobutylmagnesium iodide to benzonitrile solution under argon

steps.description: The reaction was heated to 50 °C for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat reaction mixture to 50 °C for 2 hours

steps.description: Upon completion the reaction was cooled to room temperature and quenched with 2M HCl (5 mL).
steps.searchable_summary: Cool and quench reaction with HCl

steps.description: The aqueous layer was extracted with ethyl acetate (3 × 20 mL).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract aqueous layer with ethyl acetate

steps.description: The organic layers were combined, dried (MgSO4), filtered, and concentrated.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine, dry, filter, and concentrate organic layers

steps.description: The resulting residue was purified by automated flash column chromatography (40 g silica, 0-100% heptane/ethyl acetate).
steps.searchable_summary: Purify residue using flash column chromatography

steps.description: In some cases further purification by reversed-phase column chromatography was required (12 g silica, 0-100% water/acetonitrile).
steps.searchable_summary: Further purify product using reversed-phase chromatography
